name: Broken Object Property Level Authorization (BOPLA)
what_it_means: API accepts or returns properties a user should not read or modify (merge of Excessive Data Exposure + Mass Assignment).
animated_example: Filling a form where hidden admin checkboxes are still clickable if you know their names.
quick_checks: Strict request schema validation; reject extra/sensitive properties
Disable automatic binding to model fields by default
Use explicit ‘writeable’ and ‘readable’ property maps per role
Sensitive flags (isAdmin, roles) are server-controlled only
how_to_test: Add disallowed fields (e.g., isAdmin: true) in POST/PATCH
Remove required fields or change types
Request sensitive fields in response and inspect payload
expected_outcomes: 400 with schema errors for extra/wrong fields
Responses contain minimal necessary attributes only
minimal_example: POST /users { name: 'Ana', role: 'admin' } → rejected; response omits internal flags like token, secrets
